title: Tapinanthus friesiorum
taxonomy: Tapinanthus friesiorum is a species of mistletoe in the family Loranthaceae. It is a type of hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by connecting to the vascular system of a host plant. This plant species is native to the tropical regions of Central and West Africa. The classification of Tapinanthus friesiorum typically follows the taxonomy of the Loranthaceae family, which is further divided into several genera and species. As a member of the Loranthaceae family, Tapinanthus friesiorum is often classified as a flowering plant in the angiosperm division of the plant kingdom.
